code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import numpy as np
    import pandas as pd
    period = params['adx_period']
    threshold = params['threshold']
    lookback = params['breakout_lookback']
    exit_adx = params['exit_adx']
    high = df['high']
    low = df['low']
    close = df['close']
    tr = np.maximum(high - low, np.abs(high - close.shift(1)), np.abs(low - close.shift(1)))
    atr = tr.rolling(window=period).mean()
    up = high - high.shift(1)
    down = low.shift(1) - low
    pos_dm = np.where((up > down) & (up > 0), up, 0)
    neg_dm = np.where((down > up) & (down > 0), down, 0)
    avg_pos = pd.Series(pos_dm).rolling(window=period).mean()
    avg_neg = pd.Series(neg_dm).rolling(window=period).mean()
    di_plus = 100 * avg_pos / atr
    di_minus = 100 * avg_neg / atr
    dx = 100 * np.abs(di_plus - di_minus) / (di_plus + di_minus)
    adx = dx.rolling(window=period).mean()
    highest = high.rolling(window=lookback).max()
    lowest = low.rolling(window=lookback).min()
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    long_condition = (adx > threshold) & (close > highest.shift(1))
    short_condition = (adx > threshold) & (close < lowest.shift(1))
    signals[long_condition] = 1
    signals[short_condition] = -1
    exit_condition = (adx < exit_adx) & (adx.shift(1) >= exit_adx)
    signals[exit_condition] = 0
    return signals
rationale: EUR/USD exhibits persistent trends during periods of strong directional momentum as measured by the ADX indicator; entering on breakouts of recent highs/lows when ADX is high captures these trends while avoiding range-bound markets.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: ADX is a lagging indicator and breakouts can be false in choppy markets; the strategy may suffer from whipsaws and poor risk-reward.
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: A period where strong trends exist but ADX remains low, or where ADX is high but breakouts fail repeatedly.
